about.title: About this service
about.intro: Powered by RecycleNow.com, this tool can be used to search and find recycling locations throughout the United Kingdom.
about.refillIntro: Powered by RecycleNow.com, this tool can be used to search and find refill locations throughout the United Kingdom.

becomeAPartner.title: Add this tool to your website
becomeAPartner.description: It’s been created as an embeddable widget that can be added to any website to help visitors discover more recycling options. For example, a beverage manufacturer could use the tool to show places to recycle drinks cans.
becomeAPartner.refillDescription: It’s been created as an embeddable widget that can be added to any website to help visitors discover more refill options.

feedback.title: Improving this service
feedback.description: We’re always looking to improve this tool and would be interested to hear about your experience whilst using it, good or bad.

brands.title: Refill brands available in this area

start.title: Discover refill options in your area
start.intro: Find places to refill food, cleaning and personal care products near you.

discover.title: Switch to refill on everyday essentials

steps.title: How refill works
steps.description: Bring your empty containers and fill up your favourites without the packaging waste.
items: Use the locator to find a refill store near you
Take along your clean, empty containers to fill up
Pick and choose what you want to refill

moreChoice.title: More choice, not less
moreChoice.description: From luxury olive oils to chocolate buttons sold by the scoop, refill shops stock an incredible variety of products.

localStore.title: Discover your local store
localStore.description: Every shop is different. The best way to find out what's available is to visit your nearest refill store, and discover their unique mix of products.

skipUnpacking.title: Skip the unpacking
skipUnpacking.description: Bring your own containers, or grab some at your refill shop, and refill straight into them. No more tedious unpacking when you get home, just pop them straight into your cupboards and onto your shelves.

simple.title: It's surprisingly simple
simple.description: Most customers become regulars within their first few visits. It's not about being perfectly green or knowing all the right things to say – it's simply a smarter, more enjoyable way to shop.

reduceWaste.title: Reduce waste
items: <bold>Cut packaging waste</bold> and help stop plastic at the source
Choose from a variety of <bold>eco-friendly alternatives</bold>
<bold>Support local business</bold> and help prevent waste.

saveMoney.title: Save Money
items: You're not paying for packaging - just the product
Buy exactly what you need, so nothing goes to waste
Find out if your store offers a loyalty scheme to give you more savings

testimonials.Fiona C: I love my local refill shop because I can buy as much or as little as I want. And I’ve really noticed a lot less packaging ending up in the recycle bin
testimonials.Aidan W: I love the variety of items… some that I can’t find anywhere else!
testimonials.Hamish F: It’s easy, I only buy as much as I need, I’m often saving money compared to the supermarkets. Not to mention the lovely community of people who run it and shop in there!
testimonials.Jen M: In addition to cutting out packaging, my refill shop stores products that I know are better for the planet and people! Win-win!
testimonials.Alex K: I like to buy from refill shops because it allows me to buy what I need and stops my cupboards getting clogged up with bits in packets that eventually get put in the bin.
testimonials.Helen P: It has saved me a small fortune! They are also great for trying a number of new products, and a perfect place to stock up on things for the house too, such as cleaning products, washing up liquid and laundry soap. Fab!!

whatCanRefill.question: What can I refill?
whatCanRefill.answerBold: You’ll be surprised by the variety!
whatCanRefill.answer: Most refill shops stock household essentials like washing-up liquid, laundry detergent, and surface cleaners alongside personal care products including shampoo, body wash, and hand soap. You'll also find an amazing selection of food items - from everyday pasta, rice, and cereals to premium oils, artisan chocolates, and specialty spices. Many shops also offer coffee beans, nuts, dried fruits, and even pet food. Think of it as your local supermarket reimagined!

doesRefillCostMore.question: Does refill cost more?
doesRefillCostMore.answerBold: Most customers are pleasantly surprised by the savings.
doesRefillCostMore.answer: While premium products might seem expensive per gram, you're not paying for fancy packaging, marketing, or brand overhead. Plus, you buy exactly what you need - no more throwing away half-used bottles or buying jumbo sizes you'll never finish. Many regular customers find they spend 10-20% less on household essentials, and you can try expensive products in small quantities before committing to larger amounts. It's like having a personal shopper who only charges you for what you actually use.

specialContainers.question: Do I need special containers to refill?
specialContainers.answerBold: Absolutely not - any clean, dry container works perfectly.
specialContainers.answer: Old jam jars, pasta sauce bottles, or containers you already have at home are ideal. Most shops also sell reusable food safe containers if you want to upgrade your storage game. Many places provide containers if you forget to bring your own, and some even offer a "container library" where you can borrow and return. The key is bringing containers that are clean and dry - the friendly staff will show you exactly what works best for different products.

makeDifference.question: Does refill really make a difference?
makeDifference.answerBold: Every small action adds up to something bigger.
makeDifference.answer: When you refill a 500ml bottle instead of buying a new one, you're preventing that plastic from being manufactured, transported, and eventually disposed of. Multiply that by your weekly shopping and the growing number of people choosing refill, and the impact becomes significant. But beyond the environmental benefits, you're supporting local businesses, reducing clutter in your home, and often saving money too.

moreWays.title: More ways to refill
moreWays.description: Refill is rapidly growing in popularity, but more than half of Brits struggle to find reuse or refill options at their regular stores. The good news is there are still plenty of ways to reduce packaging waste, even if your everyday supermarket.

shopConcentrate.title: Shop Concentrate
shopConcentrate.description: Lots of brands now offer concentrated versions of common bathroom and cleaning products - just add water!

homeDelivery.title: Remember the milkman?
homeDelivery.subtitle: Get essentials delivered to your door, and return the packaging to be reused!

items: Browse online and order your products
Your products arrive at your door in returnable and refillable packaging
Empty your products into your own containers and cupboards, and give the returnable packaging a rinse
Return the empty packaging so it can be used again

abelAndCole.name: Abel & Cole
abelAndCole.description: Get your everyday essentials delivered in returnable, refillable packaging. It's zero-waste shopping with a difference.

beautyKitchen.name: Beauty Kitchen
beautyKitchen.description: Return, refill, repeat scheme for beauty and personal care products. Send back empties for store credit.

milkAndMore.name: Milk & More
milkAndMore.description: Traditional doorstep delivery service bringing milk and juice in refillable bottles which are collected and reused.

theModernMilkman.name: The Modern Milkman
theModernMilkman.description: A doorstep delivery and collection service for fresh products. Their glass bottles and pots are made to come back.

yoyoGrocery.name: Yoyo Grocery
yoyoGrocery.description: Yoyo Grocery delivers natural and organic food directly to your door with a closed-loop packaging return system.

ocado.name: Ocado
ocado.description: Ocado offer an online reusable packaging scheme in a select number of locations across the UK.

place.title: You can refill <categories></categories> products at this place
place.disclaimer: Stock varies by store, so it's worth checking availability before you make the trip.

marksAndSpencer.name: Marks and Spencer
marksAndSpencer.urlText: Learn more about Marks and Spencer's refill scheme
marksAndSpencer.description: M&S Milton Keynes is offering a refill scheme with 10 own-brand home and personal care products. You pay a £2 deposit for a returnable bottle, and when you return it after use, you receive a £2 voucher towards your next Refilled purchase—making it easy to reduce plastic waste with trusted products.

ecover.name: Ecover
ecover.description: Ecover is a leading brand of eco-friendly cleaning products, offering a range of refillable household cleaners that are designed to be effective while minimizing environmental impact.

bioD.name: Bio-D
bioD.description: Bio-D creates a comprehensive range of effective, plant-based cleaning products for home and laundry, all hypoallergenic, cruelty-free and made with ethically sourced, biodegradable ingredients that are kinder to sensitive skin and the planet.

suma.name: Suma
suma.description: Suma provide a wide selection of natural, organic and vegetarian food and drink, a responsibly-sourced household range and gorgeous cruelty-free body care collection.

sesi.name: Sesi
sesi.description: SESI offers an extensive range of vegan, cruelty-free cleaning detergents and refillable household products, all formulated with biodegradable ingredients.

faithInNature.name: Faith in Nature
faithInNature.description: Faith in Nature are a personal care brand that creates plant-based, cruelty-free products. Founded in 1974, the company focuses on sustainable practices and ethical sourcing whilst avoiding harsh chemicals in their formulations.

yoyoGrocery.name: Yoyo Grocery
yoyoGrocery.description: Yoyo Grocery offers natural and organic food in store with a closed-loop packaging return system.
